title: Performance Scan
findings: Found one high-signal performance issue: the store-era recall path repeatedly walks and reads the full read-set store corpus during a single recall/section fetch.

issues_found.title: Store-era recall recomputes and rereads the full read-set corpus multiple times per request
issues_found.description: The hot recall path computes the store corpus revision, then separately builds raw candidates, and get_sections repeats the revision plus body-index build. All four calls funnel through walkReadSetStores(), which walks readKnowledgeAcrossStores() and readFileSyncs every store markdown file, so a normal fab_recall -> fab_get_knowledge_sections round trip can perform four full read-set walks/full-file reads before reading selected bodies again. Cost scales with total mounted store corpus size on every request despite the code already having the in-memory selection token and corpus revision opportunities for reuse.
issues_found.fix_direction: Build the read-set corpus once per request (or cache it by store directory fingerprint/max mtime) and thread the entries/revision/body index through plan-context and knowledge-sections; at minimum derive computeReadSetRevision, raw items, and body index from a shared walk result so each store file is read once.
